stem: A clinical research study measures treatment response rate across 5 trial sites, each containing multiple patient cohorts. trial site A has 15 observations with mean 55.9 % (within-trial site SD = 3.5). trial site B has 8 observations with mean 70.4 % (within-trial site SD = 8.2). trial site C has 8 observations with mean 43.6 % (within-trial site SD = 9.7). trial site D has 10 observations with mean 62.6 % (within-trial site SD = 9.9). trial site E has 8 observations with mean 71.8 % (within-trial site SD = 4.2). The between-trial site standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 6.9 and 17.2 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true treatment response rate for trial site C, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-trial site variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for trial site C: 45.0 % (shrunk from raw 43.6). Range [44.2, 46.9] as between-trial site SD varies from 6.9 to 17.2. Pooled mean: 60.2.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 60.2 % (weighted average of 5 trial site means).
Shrinkage factor for trial site C: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/8). At σ_between=11.5: B = 0.918.
Interval [44.2, 46.9] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (43.6) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (60.2) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
